channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 2 hours.

item.title: 🌍 How the Iran war is affecting Latin America and the Caribbean’s economic outlook | Atlantic Council
item.description: The data show how energy and commodity shortages are pushing inflation higher, tightening financial conditions, and slowing growth. The post How the Iran war is affecting Latin America and the Caribbean’s economic outlook appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🔐 Battery-electric freight trains would lift Australia’s energy independence | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Australia has an obvious way to reduce its vulnerability to disruption of fuel supplies: making battery-electric locomotives the standard means of freight-rail traction and, through resulting cost savings, draw more long-distance goods transportation onto trains. ...

item.title: 🕵️ Space Force boosts contract ceiling for Andromeda space monitoring sats to $6.2B | Breaking Defense
item.description: The Andromeda IDIQ contract vehicle will fund a replacement for the current GSSAP on-orbit inspection satellites as well as the classified SILENTBARKER space surveillance birds.

item.title: 🌍 Washington Is Still Chasing the Perfect War | Foreign Policy
item.description: Iran shows that the delusions that caused Iraq and Afghanistan persist.

item.title: 🌍 The Iran War Is Eroding Israel’s Nuclear Ambiguity | Foreign Policy
item.description: Officially or not, Washington is now breaking the taboo and talking about its ally’s bombs.

item.title: 🌏 Why Did Beijing Kill a $2 Billion AI Deal? | Foreign Policy
item.description: The flow of global capital is hitting new walls.

item.title: 📍 B-1B “Apocalypse II” Out Of The Boneyard And Back In Service | The War Zone
item.description: After nearly two years of work, the Bone once known as Rage has completed its journey out of the desert and back to active service. The post B-1B “Apocalypse II” Out Of The Boneyard And Back In Service appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🕵️ Coast Guard unveils new Special Missions Command amid demand for elite units | Breaking Defense
item.description: The Coast Guard aims to officially stand up the command in October.

item.title: 📍 Can a U.S.-Brokered State Budget Help Unite Libya? | Foreign Policy
item.description: Inside what analysts call a “pinky promise” between Libya’s rival factions.

item.title: 📍 Unlocking the next source of US energy dominance | Atlantic Council
item.description: The US is ramping up its fusion research to bridge the gap between what's possible and what's affordable, reliable, and secure. The post Unlocking the next source of US energy dominance appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 DARPA’s XRQ-73 Hybrid-Electric Flying Wing Drone Has Flown | The War Zone
item.description: The XRQ-73 was designed with a focus on very quiet, high-efficiency flight, and it has evolved since it was last seen in 2024. The post DARPA’s XRQ-73 Hybrid-Electric Flying Wing Drone Has Flown appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 US forces fire at, disable Iran-flagged tanker trying to evade blockade | Defense News
item.description: While President Trump said Project Freedom was paused, the U.S. Navy blockade continues to ban vessels from entering and exiting Iranian ports.

item.title: ⚛️ Turkey rolls out intercontinental missile with purported 6,000km range | Breaking Defense
item.description: The ICBM would add a “crucial layer of deterrence” to Turkey’s military arsenal, a defense analyst told Breaking Defense.

item.title: 🌍 France moves aircraft carrier to Red Sea with eye on Hormuz mission | Defense News
item.description: France on Wednesday deployed its carrier strike group to the Red Sea as part of planning for a potential mission to secure the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: 🏛️ B-52 engine replacement clears critical design review, first modifications to begin this year | Defense News
item.description: The Air Force’s program to replace the B-52H Stratofortress’s 1960s-era engines cleared its critical design review, the service announced May 4.

item.title: 📍 Lockheed opposes Northrop bid to remove firewall on solid rocket motor business | Breaking Defense
item.description: Northrop argues that a 2018 consent order is hampering its ability to ramp up solid rocket motor production needed for critical munitions.

item.title: 🌏 Missiles Clobber Target Ship In Highly Strategic Luzon Strait | The War Zone
item.description: The ex-Philippine Navy warship was sent to the bottom during high-profile Luzon Strait drills and amid rising Indo-Pacific tensions. The post Missiles Clobber Target Ship In Highly Strategic Luzon Strait appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏴 How Much Longer Can Russia Last in the War? | War on the Rocks
item.description: In 2025, Collin Meisel and Mathew Burrows wrote, “Russia Can Afford to Take a Beating in Ukraine,” where they argued Russia was able to absorb the blows Ukraine was delivering and could continue fighting for a while. A year later, we asked Collin and Mathew to revisit their…

item.title: 🌏 Is the Iran War Pushing Southeast Asia into China’s Arms? | Foreign Policy
item.description: U.S. guarantees have not shielded Indonesia, the Philippines, and Vietnam from economic shock.

item.title: ⚛️ Malaysia looks for ‘further clarifications’ from Norway after Naval Strike Missile order falters | Breaking Defense
item.description: Malaysia and Kongsberg signed a deal in 2018 for supply of an undisclosed number of NSM weapons to equip six new Littoral Combat Ships.

item.title: 🔐 Outgoing Leonardo boss touts ‘Michelangelo Dome,’ cyber tack as key achievements | Defense News
item.description: Asked what he plans to do next, Cingolani said, “I am getting on my motorbike and will travel around Europe for a while.”

item.title: 🏴 Ukrainian battlefield gains expose Russia’s communications problems | Atlantic Council
item.description: Ukraine has demonstrated in recent months that it is capable of exploiting weaknesses in Russia’s front line defenses. Kyiv’s allies should now seek to prioritize the tools that will identify and exacerbate these weak points, writes Miro Sedlák. The post Ukrainian battlefield…

item.title: 🌍 France moves carrier into Red Sea ahead of potential Hormuz mission | Defense News
item.description: President Emmanuel Macron has said any multinational initiative to secure the strait would only happen once the hottest phase of the conflict subsides.

item.title: 📍 Leonardo reports 31% surge in new orders in Q1 as CEO departs | Breaking Defense
item.description: The defense boss highlighted that revenues reached €4.5 billion in the first three months of the year — a 7 percent increase over 2025 — with a total backlog reaching €57 billion.

item.title: 🏴 American strategic bases in Europe ‘not yet’ in danger: German president | Breaking Defense
item.description: “The only signal we can give, and we should give: The less [the] USA is present in Europe with troops, the more should be done by Europe,” Bundespräsident Frank-Walter Steinmeier said during a visit to Sweden.

item.title: 🏛️ AMPV 30 heading to TiC 2.0 with hopes to ‘get the Army thinking’: Kongsberg exec | Breaking Defense
item.description: The Army said the AMPV 30 is not a replacement option for the XM30 program, but an executive said the vehicle “fits a different envelope.”

item.title: 🌏 The United States’ Korea Strategy Is Working Against Itself | Foreign Policy
item.description: By pulling Seoul into its rivalry with Beijing, Washington is undermining the alliance’s core mission.

item.title: 🏛️ Army wants to field all 11 divisions NGC2 capabilities in five-year window | Breaking Defense
item.description: I Corps, headquartered in the Pacific, will be the next focus for NGC2 next year.

item.title: 🌏 China and Japan Are Entering a More Dangerous Phase of Rivalry | The Diplomat
item.description: Neither China nor Japan wants war. The danger is that each side increasingly believes the other is preparing for one.

item.title: 🌍 Iraq’s Continuing Struggle for Sovereignty | Foreign Policy
item.description: Amid a regional war, political drama and drone strikes are testing Baghdad’s power.
